medications.indication_rationale: Not appropriate to start via telehealth for a self-limiting anxiety/globus episode.
Patient is euthyroid; no replacement is required.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stop palpating the neck, take small sips of cool water, and slow the breathing until the heart rate settles.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces the muscle tension and hyperventilation driving the globus sensation.

patient_education_points.point: Your airway is open — you can talk in full sentences, drink water, and there's no noisy breathing. The tight, lump feeling is muscle tension from anxiety, not your thyroid suddenly swelling.
A stable thyroid goitre changes over years, not minutes. Stop pressing on your neck (it can bruise and worsen the feeling) and slow your breathing.
Go to ED or call 000 if you develop noisy/whistling breathing (stridor), your voice suddenly becomes very hoarse, you can't swallow liquids at all, or your lips turn blue.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Psychological support for health anxiety, thyroid ultrasound/TFT review.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Stridor, sudden hoarseness, inability to swallow liquids, or cyanosis — ED/000.

must_include_items: Confirm airway patency via voice/speech/swallow and absence of stridor before reassuring
Explain globus as anxiety-related muscle tension over a stable goitre
Coach breathing and stop neck palpation
acceptable_alternatives: Routine GP follow-up for anxiety and thyroid monitoring
errors_of_commission: Dispatching a lights-and-sirens ambulance for a patent airway
Reinforcing the belief that the thyroid is acutely closing the airway
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T1 case: passing requires an airway-patency check + reassurance + stridor safety-net. Ambulance dispatch for a patent airway is over-triage.
